summary: Typosquat of big.js with hidden load-time require of unrelated sibling package
details: Package ships a verbatim copy of MikeMcl's big.js library (preserving original author, copyright, and repo URL) under the unrelated name `new-ecro`. Both the CommonJS entry (big.js:606) and ESM entry (big.mjs:606) insert a try/catch wrapper that calls `require("parket-slot")` and invokes `doc.from_str()` at module load — fired automatically on `require('new-ecro')` or `import 'new-ecro'`. The package declares a single runtime dependency `new-solt@0.0.9`, which is unrelated to arbitrary-precision arithmetic, shares the `new-<word>` naming shape with the host package, and is pinned to the same version — consistent with coordinated typosquat publishing where the sibling package carries the actual payload. The load-time require pattern combined with an impersonated identity, an undeclared module name (`parket-slot`), and a same-version sibling dependency is a deliberate dropper attack-graph rather than benign packaging.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
